title: ulmus minor subsp. angustifolia
intro: Ulmus minor subsp. angustifolia, also known as the narrow-leaved elm or wych elm, is a subspecies of the common elm tree (Ulmus minor) native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Ulmus minor subsp. angustifolia include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system, making it a valuable remedy for colds, coughs, and sore throats. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for a range of uses in traditional medicine and natural remedies.
